Notice Of Motion And Motion To Hold Defendant In Contemptand For Discovery Sanctions For Willful Disobedience To An Order Of This Court
Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. VAL D. HORNSTEIN, Esq. (Tel: 415-454-1490) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF HORNSTEIN LAW OFFICES, PROF. CORP's Motion To Hold Defendant In Contempt and For Discovery Sanctions For Willful Disobedience To An Order Of This Court.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Continued To Apr-06-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Order Eastablishing Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM videoconference:
Val D. Hornstein, Esq., Tel. 415.454.1490, for the Plaintiff;
William P. O'Connell, Esq., Tel. 415.485.2200, for the Defendant.

Mr. Hornstein stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem.

Matter called. Mr. O'Connell did not give notice of contesting the tentative ruling. Mr. Hornstein objects to any argument by Mr. O'Connell.

Judge Pro Tem Fleming states that no notice was given to oppose the tentative ruling, and that it cannot hear Mr. O'Connell in fairness and due process to the other side.

The tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF HORNSTEIN LAW OFFICES, PROF. CORP's Motion For Order Eastablishing Admissions And For Sanctions is adopted as follows:

The motion is unopposed and is granted. Sanctions are awarded against defendant William P. O'Connell in the amount of $2475 due within 15 days of date of order and otherwise denied. Counsel to moving party shall submit a proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: J. Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Defendant'S Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Nov-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM videoconference:
Val D. Hornstein, Esq., Tel. 415.454.1490, for the Plaintiff;
William P. O'Connell, Esq., Tel. 415.485.2200, for the Defendant.

Mr. Hornstein stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem.

Matter called. Mr. O'Connell did not give notice of contesting the tentative ruling. Mr. Hornstein objects to any argument by Mr. O'Connell.

Judge Pro Tem Fleming states that no notice was given to oppose the tentative ruling, and that it cannot hear Mr. O'Connell in fairness and due process to the other side.

The tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF HORNSTEIN LAW OFFICES, PROF. CORP's Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Discovery is adopted as follows:

The motion is unopposed and will be granted. Sanctions are awarded against defendant William P. O'Connell in the amount of $2589 due within 15 days of date of order and otherwise denied. Counsel to moving party shall submit a proposed order in Word format to the Temporary Judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: J. Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
